chapter_title: Special woven fabrics; tufted textile fabrics; lace; tapestries; trimmings; embroidery
scope_note: Note 1: Ch.58 does NOT apply to Ch.59 fabrics (impregnated/coated/laminated — even those with Ch.58 base fabrics). Note 7: Ch.58 includes articles of metal thread of a kind used in apparel/furnishing (in addition to heading 58.09 products).
inclusions: Heading 58.01 — Woven pile fabrics and chenille fabrics, other than fabrics of heading 58.02 or 58.06, including weft pile fabrics not yet cut (Note 2). Velvet, plush, corduroy
Heading 58.02 — Terry towelling and similar woven terry fabrics, other than narrow woven fabrics of heading 58.06; tufted textile fabrics, other than products of heading 57.03

Heading 58.03 — Gauze, other than narrow fabrics of heading 58.06. Note 3: 'gauze' = fabric with warp of standing/ground threads AND crossing/doup threads making half or full turns to form loops through which weft passes

Heading 58.04 — Tulles and other net fabrics, not including woven, knitted or crocheted fabrics; lace in the piece, in strips or in motifs, other than fabrics of headings 60.02 to 60.06. Note 4: 58.04 does NOT cover knotted net fabrics of twine/cordage/rope (→ 56.08)
Heading 58.05 — Hand-woven tapestries of the type Gobelins, Flanders, Aubusson, Beauvais and the like, and needle-worked tapestries (for example, petit point, cross stitch), whether or not made up

Heading 58.06 — Narrow woven fabrics, other than goods of heading 58.07. Note 5: 'narrow woven fabrics' = (a) woven fabrics ≤30 cm width with selvedges on both edges (woven-as-such or cut from wider pieces); (b) tubular woven fabrics with flattened width ≤30 cm; (c) bias binding with folded edges, unfolded width ≤30 cm. Narrow fabrics WITH woven fringes → heading 58.08
Heading 58.07 — Labels, badges and similar articles of textile material, in the piece, in strips or cut to shape or size, not embroidered

Heading 58.08 — Braids in the piece; ornamental trimmings in the piece, without embroidery, other than knitted or crocheted; tassels, pompons and similar articles. Also includes narrow woven fabrics with woven fringes (per Note 5 last paragraph)

Heading 58.09 — Woven fabrics of metal thread and woven fabrics of metallised yarn of heading 56.05, of a kind used in apparel, as furnishing fabrics or for similar purposes, not elsewhere specified or included

Heading 58.10 — Embroidery in the piece, in strips or in motifs. Note 6: 'embroidery' includes embroidery with metal/glass thread on visible textile ground; and sewn appliqué work of sequins, beads or ornamental motifs of textile or other materials. Excludes needlework tapestry (→ 58.05)
Heading 58.11 — Quilted textile products in the piece, composed of one or more layers of textile materials assembled with padding by stitching or otherwise, other than embroidery of heading 58.10

exclusions.condition: Ch.58 fabrics that are impregnated, coated, covered or laminated (other than heading 58.11 items referenced in Ch.59 Notes)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 59 — Note 1. A pile fabric coated with plastics → 59.03 (excepting Ch.60 Note 1(c) for knitted pile). Gauze impregnated → 59.07. The coating moves the fabric from Ch.58 to Ch.59

exclusions.condition: Knotted net fabrics of twine, cordage or rope
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 56.08 (knotted netting) — Note 4. Fishing nets, cargo nets made by knotting → 56.08. Tulle and net fabrics produced by twisting threads without knotting → 58.04

exclusions.condition: Needlework tapestry (petit point, cross stitch) classified as embroidery
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 58.05 — Note 6. Needlework tapestries that could be described as embroidery → always 58.05. Hand-woven tapestries (Gobelins, Aubusson, Flanders type) → 58.05

exclusions.condition: Tufted textile fabrics of a kind used as floor coverings (carpet-type tufted)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 57.03 (tufted textile floor coverings) — Note 58.02 scope excludes 57.03 products. Tufted carpeting → 57.03. Tufted terry towelling → 58.02

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — Coated/impregnated Ch.58 fabrics migrate to Ch.59
classification_rules.description: When a fabric that would otherwise be classified in Ch.58 (pile, gauze, lace, tapestry, trimming) is IMPREGNATED, COATED, COVERED or LAMINATED, it moves to Chapter 59. Exception: knitted pile fabrics (velour, terry) that are coated/impregnated → stay in heading 60.01 per Ch.60 Note 1(c). So: woven pile fabric coated → Ch.59. Knitted pile fabric coated → Ch.60 (60.01). This exception does NOT apply to Ch.58 woven pile.

classification_rules.rule: Note 5 — 'Narrow woven fabrics' (58.06) definition: width ≤30 cm with selvedges or special construction
classification_rules.description: Heading 58.06 covers: (a) woven fabrics of width ≤30 cm with selvedges on BOTH edges (whether woven to width or cut from wider fabric — but must have selvedges); (b) tubular woven fabrics, flattened width ≤30 cm; (c) bias binding, unfolded width ≤30 cm. EXCLUSION from 58.06: narrow woven fabrics WITH WOVEN FRINGES → heading 58.08 instead. 58.06 is for plain-edge narrow fabrics; fringed → 58.08.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — Gauze (58.03) vs plain open-weave fabrics vs net fabrics
classification_rules.description: Heading 58.03 covers 'gauze' in the specific technical sense: warp threads with CROSSING/DOUP threads that make half or complete turns forming loops enclosing weft threads. This is a characteristic leno weave construction. Plain open-weave fabrics (not leno/gauze construction) → classified in the appropriate Ch.50–55 woven fabric heading by fibre. Net fabrics not made by leno weave → 58.04 (tulle, net). Medical surgical gauze (plain open weave, not leno) → may be Ch.52 or Ch.63.07 depending on form.

classification_rules.rule: Note 6 — Embroidery (58.10) vs tapestries (58.05): sewn-on decoration vs hand-worked/woven
classification_rules.description: Two headings for decorative textile work: 58.05 = TAPESTRIES (hand-woven: Gobelins/Aubusson/Flanders/Beauvais types; AND needle-worked: petit point, cross stitch — worked with needle on ground fabric, creating the design BY the needlework itself). 58.10 = EMBROIDERY (decorative STITCHING applied to a VISIBLE GROUND FABRIC — the ground remains visible; includes metal/glass thread embroidery, and sewn APPLIQUÉ work of sequins/beads/motifs). Note 6 explicitly: embroidery excludes needlework tapestry (→ 58.05). Key test: does the ground fabric remain VISIBLE under the work? Yes → embroidery (58.10). Is it petit point/cross stitch creating the image from scratch? → tapestry (58.05).

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 58.01 (woven pile) vs 58.02 (terry/tufted) vs Ch.57 (carpet tufted) vs Ch.60 (knitted pile)
classification_rules.description: Pile/terry fabrics in multiple chapters: 58.01 = WOVEN PILE fabrics (velvet, plush, corduroy — pile formed during weaving by warp or weft float); includes uncut weft pile. 58.02 = TERRY WOVEN fabrics + TUFTED fabrics (pile inserted into base fabric by tufting needle) EXCEPT floor covering tufted (57.03). Ch.57 (57.03) = TUFTED textile floor coverings (carpets). Ch.60 (60.01) = KNITTED or CROCHETED pile fabrics (velour fleece, knitted terry). The construction method (woven pile vs. tufted vs. knitted pile) and intended use (floor covering vs. apparel/furnishing) determine the heading.
